column1: Jacob Wilson leads the charge for Beat The Streak with a steady bat at the top of Colorado’s lineup, boasting a sky-high 74.5% hit probability and the added bonus of a hitter-friendly park on the road. Manny Machado follows closely, locked into the two-hole at home and flashing a strong matchup thanks to a clear platoon advantage against Joe La Sorsa, whose numbers suggest plenty of opportunity for contact. Fernando Tatis Jr. and Luke Keaschall round out the list, each offering reliable profiles in favorable lineup spots for those looking to keep their streaks alive. This slate is loaded with top-order bats in spots built for hits—don’t overthink it.
